Construction, 2023. Accessed: 2025-04-28' (same citation format the paper uses for other Roboflow-Universe dataset sources, e.g. refs [4],[7],[10]). No embedded hyperlink/URI was recoverable from the PDF (checked via PDF /URI annotation extraction -- only the arXiv self-link was embedded), so the EXACT resolvable URL is [VERIFY] pending VERIFIER confirmation. But the paper's own explicit textual claim of public code+dataset availability is full-text evidence that the original d7=0 rationale ('no GitHub/HF mentioned in briefing') no longer holds -- this was a briefing-visibility gap, not a paper gap. Scored d7=1 (informal/non-archival data-link sub-case per d7_rubric_codebook.md), not d7=2, since no license or archival-repo confirmation was found. d8=0 (unchanged): no explicit contamination/leakage safeguard; if anything, paper states images were deliberately reused from 'prior available datasets... commonly used in the field' rather than newly collected, which is a mild leakage-risk signal, not a safeguard. d9=0 (unchanged, re-checked carefully): the paper's ground truth IS human-hand-labeled, but this is standard annotation, not a separate 'human baseline' performance metric benchmarked against the models in the same evaluation framework (unlike R037) -- no distinct human-performance score is reported anywhere. d10=1 (unchanged): worker monitoring has real safety/productivity deployment value; emotion recognition framed as more research-level than operational. bibliographic.paper_title: PIDQA—Question Answering on Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
bibliographic.authors: Mohit Gupta, Chialing Wei, Thomas Czerniawski, Ricardo Eiris
bibliographic.venue: Machine Learning and Knowledge Extraction (MDPI), Vol. 7, Issue 2, Article 39
bibliographic.dataset_url: https://github.com/mgupta70/PIDQA (QA pairs + Cypher queries, CC0-1.0); underlying P&ID images from the pre-existing public 'Dataset-P&ID' (Paliwal et al. 2021, Digitize-PID), referenced not re-hosted
bibliographic.version_accessed: MDPI OA PDF, RESEARCHER 2026-07-12, published 21 April 2025

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Process/industrial-plant engineering documentation — Piping & Instrumentation Diagram (P&ID) question answering, adjacent to AEC/built-environment (BORDERLINE, see _screening_rationale). Authors affiliated with Built Environment school (ASU).
domain_use_case.lifecycle_stage: Design review / operations (material takeoffs, safety-compliance verification, CAD-drafting-error detection on existing P&ID documentation)
domain_use_case.intended_user: Process/piping engineers, EPC (Engineering-Procurement-Construction) design reviewers, plant safety auditors, AEC/process-engineering AI researchers

task.task_type: Natural-language question answering over P&ID diagrams via LLM-generated Cypher graph queries on a Labeled Property Graph (LPG) representation. 4 question categories: (I) simple counting, (II) spatial counting, (III) spatial connections (multi-hop, safety-check style), (IV) value-based queries (tag/attribute lookup, CAD-error detection)
task.task_complexity: Structured graph-query generation (text-to-Cypher) grounded on a P&ID knowledge graph; complexity varies by question type (multi-hop spatial-connection queries hardest, per Table 4 accuracy spread)
task.output_type: Cypher query -> executed graph-query result -> LLM-reformatted natural-language answer (count / boolean / entity list)

modality.notes: Upstream pipeline (Section 3.2.1, not itself LLM-evaluated) digitizes raw P&ID IMAGES into a graph via YOLOv11/KerasOCR/PHT; the LLM-evaluated QA stage (Section 3.2.3, PIDQA benchmark proper) operates entirely on TEXT (natural-language question) + a structured/symbolic Labeled Property Graph representation (Cypher schema), not on raw images. engineering_drawings=true reflects the P&ID source artifact; symbolic_layout=true reflects the graph/Cypher structured representation the LLM actually consumes.

dataset_properties.n_samples: 64,000 QA pairs (128 questions x 500 P&ID sheets: 300 train / 100 val / 100 test)
dataset_properties.n_tasks_categories: 4 question categories (simple counting, spatial counting, spatial connections, value-based), each with 20 linguistically-diverse templates (16,000 questions/category)
dataset_properties.data_source: Programmatically generated QA pairs (via gpt-3.5-turbo-authored templates) over the pre-existing public synthetic 'Dataset-P&ID' (Paliwal et al. 2021, Digitize-PID) — 500 synthetic P&ID sheets, 32 symbol classes (numeric IDs only, no human-readable names)
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Programmatic/template-based generation from ground-truth graph structure (deterministic, rule-derived from the synthetic dataset's known symbol/line/text labels), not manual expert annotation of individual QA pairs
dataset_properties.expert_validation: Base entity graph construction included a semi-automatic human-in-the-loop error-correction step (Section 3.2.1); the 64k QA pairs themselves are template-generated, not individually expert-reviewed
dataset_properties.license: CC0-1.0 (GitHub repo, confirmed) for PIDQA QA data + code; article itself CC BY 4.0 (MDPI OA)
dataset_properties.accessibility: Public, no paywall: GitHub github.com/mgupta70/PIDQA (confirmed live, 14 stars/3 forks) + MDPI OA article + explicit Data Availability Statement
dataset_properties.train_test_split: 300 train / 100 validation / 100 test P&ID sheets (entity-recognition stage); QA evaluation reported on the test-set sheets
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: [VERIFY] Not discussed in paper. QA pairs are newly generated (2025) reducing direct leakage risk, but the underlying Dataset-P&ID base images are from a 2021 public prior-work dataset that could plausibly appear in later LLM pretraining corpora — undiscussed.

evaluation.metrics: Accuracy (exact Cypher-execution-result match) per question category and context level
evaluation.baseline_models: gpt-3.5-turbo ONLY — single model, evaluated across 4 prompting/context conditions (this is a context-ablation study of one model, not a multi-model comparison)
evaluation.prompting_method: 4-level context-grounding ablation: Level 0 (no schema context) / Level 1 (basic graph schema) / Level 2 (schema + attribute-value statistics) / Level 3 (Level 2 + dynamic 3-shot examples)
evaluation.rag_tool_use: Schema-grounding + dynamic few-shot retrieval (not RAG over documents; retrieval is over graph-schema exemplars) + tool use (Cypher execution on Neo4j graph database)
evaluation.llm_as_judge_method: None (code-based exact-match evaluation via Cypher query execution against ground truth)
evaluation.robustness_tests: Linguistic-diversity robustness across paraphrased question templates (implicit in the 20-template design); qualitative failure-mode analysis (schema hallucination, referential ambiguity)

capability.task_spans: Output Quality (structured graph-grounded QA accuracy on P&ID Cypher queries)
capability.modality_spans: 4.1 Text (natural-language questions/answers) + 4.6 Structured/Symbolic (Labeled Property Graph / Cypher schema representation the LLM actually consumes; underlying P&ID source is 2D Visual/engineering-drawing but is digitized by a separate non-LLM upstream pipeline, not directly LLM-consumed — disclosed, consistent with this record's own modality.notes)
capability.evaluation_spans: Code-based (exact Cypher-execution-result match, per-category accuracy, Table 4)
capability.capability_spans: Domain Knowledge (AEC/process-plant P&ID instance) + Tool Use (Cypher query generation + execution on Neo4j graph database) + Planning & Reasoning (multi-hop spatial-connection queries)
capability.building_system_label: B4 Process Equipment (Div 40-49) + B2 Facility Services (Div 20-29)
capability.profession_note: Source: CURATOR benchmark_records.jsonl taxonomy_v2 — Dim5/Dim6 confirmed from full-text (or CURATOR abstract fields for paywalled records).

rubric._notes: FULL-TEXT SCORED 2026-07-12 (RESEARCHER, PDF read at Stage-2 INCLUDE time, not a briefing-provisional score). d1=1: P&ID is a real engineering-drawing artifact, but this is process/industrial-plant engineering (oil-refinery/petroleum-industry worked examples: OISD-129, ASME B31.3), adjacent to core AEC not core AEC itself (see _screening_rationale C1 borderline); additionally the underlying dataset is SYNTHETIC (Dataset-P&ID), not real facility drawings. d2=2: task types (material takeoffs, safety-compliance spatial-connection checks citing real engineering logic e.g. 'strainers upstream of pumps', CAD-drafting-error detection) are realistic professional P&ID-engineer tasks with concrete worked examples. d3=1: LLM-evaluated stage is text+structured-graph only (no raw image modality reaches the LLM; image digitization is a separate non-LLM upstream pipeline) — single primary modality. d4=1: QA pairs are programmatically template-generated from ground-truth graph structure (internally consistent/deterministic) but not individually expert-authored/audited; symbol classes are numeric-only with no human-readable names (self-disclosed Limitation #2). d5=2: evaluation protocol is fully specified and reproducible — 4 clearly-defined context levels, deterministic temperature=0, per-category accuracy table (Table 4), qualitative failure analysis. d6=0: only ONE model evaluated (gpt-3.5-turbo) across 4 prompting conditions — this is a single-model context-ablation, not a baseline comparison; fails the 'diverse frontier + open-source baselines' bar entirely (no GPT-4/Claude/Gemini/open-source comparison) — this is the floor-failing dimension. d7=2: PIDQA (QA pairs + Cypher + eval code) is on GitHub under CC0-1.0, confirmed live and public, with an explicit MDPI Data Availability Statement — full public canonical release, no access barriers. d8=0: no explicit contamination/leakage discussion; underlying base images (Dataset-P&ID, 2021) predate many LLM training cutoffs, undiscussed risk. d9=0: no human baseline reported anywhere. d10=2: directly tied to real P&ID-engineering practice (MTOs, safety compliance, drafting-error detection) with concrete cited engineering-standard examples. SUM CHECK: 1+2+1+1+2+0+2+0+0+2=11, matches total=11.

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Power transmission-line physical-asset inspection (power/utility infrastructure vertical, in scope per the 2026-07-12 overturn)

domain_use_case.intended_user: Power-utility inspection engineers/technicians conducting transmission-line hazard/foreign-object inspection

task.task_type: Visual dialogue/VQA for power transmission-line inspection: detailed image description, multi-round conversation, and complex-reasoning hazard assessment (e.g. kite/debris entanglement risk); evaluated via the PowerQA multiple-choice benchmark (2-4 options/question)
task.task_complexity: 3 conversation types (detailed description, long conversation, complex reasoning) in training data; eval benchmark is single-turn MC-format zero-shot testing
task.output_type: Free-text dialogue response (model); letter/option selection scored against a reference answer (PowerQA eval)

modality.notes: Single image + text-dialogue modality throughout — real-world power transmission-line photographs paired with natural-language instructions/questions and generated text responses. No video, 3D, tabular, or engineering-drawing modality involved despite the physical-infrastructure subject matter.

dataset_properties.n_samples: Pre-training: 608K image-text pairs (558K from public LLaVA release + 50K newly generated). Instruction-tuning: 100K samples (22K detailed description + 45K conversation + 33K complex reasoning). PowerQA eval benchmark: 1,000 MC-format samples.
dataset_properties.n_tasks_categories: 3 instruction-data types (detailed description / long conversation / complex reasoning) for training; PowerQA eval is a single unified MC-QA task format
dataset_properties.data_source: 20,000 real-world power-transmission-line images collected by the authors; captions from VL-model captioners (VisualGLM, InternLM-XComposer-VL, Qwen-VL-Chat, ShareGPT4V) + object-detection labels (Co-DETR); instruction-tuning conversations generated by ChatGPT from those captions/labels; PowerQA questions/answers generated by GPT-4V from real inspection images across multiple hazard perspectives
dataset_properties.annotation_method: Semi-automated pipeline: VL-model captioning + detector labels + ChatGPT-generated conversation synthesis (training data, not individually human-authored); PowerQA benchmark specifically underwent a manual refinement pass to remove ambiguous/dirty samples ('all samples are refined manually')
dataset_properties.expert_validation: PowerQA underwent manual human refinement, but the paper does not state the reviewers were domain (electrical-engineering) experts specifically — reviewer qualifications are unstated, disclosed as a gap rather than assumed
dataset_properties.license: Not stated anywhere in the accessible text
dataset_properties.accessibility: NOT confirmed public as of this paper — the text states only 'Code shall be released' (future tense in the abstract); no GitHub/HuggingFace/download URL is given anywhere in the PDF. Disclosed as unreleased-at-time-of-publication, not assumed public.
dataset_properties.train_test_split: 608K pre-training + 100K instruction-tuning (no held-out split described for these); PowerQA (1,000 samples) functions purely as a zero-shot eval set, no train/test split within it
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: [VERIFY] Not discussed in the paper. PowerQA is GPT-4V-generated from the authors' own 2024 image collection, reducing direct pretraining-corpus overlap risk for contemporaneous models, but this is not explicitly analyzed by the authors.

evaluation.metrics: Accuracy (%) on PowerQA MC questions
evaluation.baseline_models: VisualGLM, InstructBLIP, MiniGPT4, InternLM-XComposer-VL, Qwen-VL-Chat, GPT-4V — 6 comparison models spanning open-source and proprietary (GPT-4V) systems
evaluation.prompting_method: Zero-shot testing on PowerQA MC questions; models directly select an answer from provided options
evaluation.human_eval_method: None as an evaluation baseline (humans were involved only in PowerQA's construction/refinement, not as benchmark-takers)
evaluation.llm_as_judge_method: None — exact-match accuracy against reference MC answers
evaluation.robustness_tests: Ablation on training-data scale (Fig. 5) and on conversation-type composition (Table 2: removing any of the 3 data types degrades performance)
evaluation.cost_latency_reporting: Training-data-scale/cost efficiency argument reported (Power-LLaVA needs only 708K samples vs. LLaVA-family models' 5M+) but no inference latency/cost figures

capability.evaluation_spans: Code-based (exact-match accuracy on PowerQA MC questions against reference answers)

bibliographic.paper_title: SubstationAI: Multimodal Large Model-Based Approaches for Analyzing Substation Equipment Faults
bibliographic.authors: Jinzhi Wang, Qinfeng Song, Lidong Qian, Haozhou Li, Qinke Peng, Jiangbo Zhang
bibliographic.venue: arXiv preprint (cs.AI); no conference/journal venue stated in the accessible text beyond the arXiv listing
bibliographic.version_accessed: arXiv v1, 22 Dec 2024. PDF on-disk, pdftotext-read in full 2026-07-12.

domain_use_case.aec_subdomain: Substation equipment fault diagnosis (power/utility infrastructure vertical, in scope per the 2026-07-12 overturn)
domain_use_case.lifecycle_stage: Operations & maintenance — fault detection, cause analysis, and repair-recommendation generation for substation equipment
domain_use_case.intended_user: Substation maintenance engineers/on-site technicians (paper explicitly frames the output as guiding 'on-site engineers in equipment maintenance and fault handling')

task.task_type: Multimodal fault-analysis-report generation from substation-equipment fault images: identifies fault type (T), analyzes cause (C), and generates repair suggestions (S) as a structured 3-part report
task.task_complexity: Structured multi-part generation task, enhanced with a retrieval-based domain-knowledge module (BERT keyword extraction + knowledge-base lookup) layered atop supervised fine-tuning
task.output_type: Free-text fault-analysis report, scored by human expert raters on a 1-5 scale across 4 custom quality dimensions (Accuracy, Clarity, Completeness, Practicality)

modality.notes: Single image+text modality — real substation-fault photographs (some video-augmented via image-to-video generation then re-extracted as still frames) paired with text prompts and generated fault-analysis reports. No engineering-drawing, 3D, or tabular modality.

dataset_properties.n_samples: Base: 10,330 real substation fault images (public source dataset, 14 fault types + location labels). Augmented to 40,000 images via image-to-video generation (EasyAnimate) + 1.5-second screenshot extraction. 1,000 samples randomly selected as the comparative benchmark/eval set.
dataset_properties.n_tasks_categories: 14 substation fault types (e.g. transformer damage, cable insulation failure, breather/silica-gel degradation); single unified report-generation task format across all types
dataset_properties.data_source: Base images from China Electric Power Research Institute's publicly available substation defect-detection dataset; fault-analysis-report ground truth generated by GPT-4 guided by prompts derived from State Grid Corporation of China's official 'Guidelines for the Assessment of the Condition of Power Transmission and Transformation Equipment'
dataset_properties.annotation_method: GPT-4-generated draft fault-analysis reports, followed by a secondary human review pass by 5 named electrical experts who corrected unreasonable elements — a genuine domain-expert-in-the-loop QC step
dataset_properties.expert_validation: YES — explicitly '5 electrical experts' reviewed/corrected the GPT-4-generated ground-truth reports, and separately '5 engineers with a background in the electric power industry' rated the models' generated reports during evaluation — real domain-expert involvement at both the annotation and evaluation stages
dataset_properties.license: Not explicitly stated for the 40K augmented dataset; the base 10,330-image dataset is described as publicly released by the source institute, but exact license terms are not given in the accessible text
dataset_properties.accessibility: NOT confirmed public — no repository/download URL for the paper's own 40K-entry augmented dataset, fault-analysis-report annotations, or trained model weights is given anywhere in the accessible text. The base 10,330-image source dataset is public, but that predates and is external to this paper's own contribution.
dataset_properties.train_test_split: LoRA fine-tuning conducted on the constructed dataset over 60 hours (train split not separately itemized); 1,000 samples randomly held out as the comparative benchmark
dataset_properties.leakage_risk: [VERIFY] Not discussed in the paper.

evaluation.metrics: Accuracy (1-5 human-rated); Clarity (1-5); Completeness (1-5); Practicality (1-5); Average of the 4
evaluation.baseline_models: GPT-4, Claude-3, LLAVA1.5-7B, VisualGLM-6B, Qwen2-VL-7B, MiniGPT-4 — 6 models spanning 2 proprietary (GPT-4, Claude-3) and 4 open-source systems
evaluation.prompting_method: Direct prompting (fault image + guiding prompt) to generate a structured fault-analysis report; ablation additionally compares SFT-only, knowledge-enhancement-only, and knowledge-enhancement-plus-Zero-shot-CoT variants
evaluation.zero_few_shot: Not zero/few-shot in the LLM-prompting sense — SubstationAI is a supervised-fine-tuned (LoRA) model; baselines evaluated presumably zero-shot/off-the-shelf (not explicitly stated for each baseline)
evaluation.rag_tool_use: Yes — a retrieval-augmented knowledge-enhancement module: BERT-based keyword extraction from the model's initial description, followed by knowledge-base lookup and integration of the highest-scoring retrieved knowledge points
evaluation.human_eval_method: YES — 5 engineers with an electric-power-industry background rated each model's generated reports against the 4 predefined criteria, a genuine human-expert evaluation protocol (not an automatic metric or LLM-as-judge)
evaluation.robustness_tests: Ablation study (Table II): Original / SFT-only / CoT-only / Knowledge-Enhancement-only / SFT+CoT / SFT+Knowledge-Enhancement combinations
evaluation.cost_latency_reporting: Training compute specified (3x RTX 3090 GPUs, 60 hours) but no inference latency/cost figures

capability.modality_spans: 4.3 2D Visual (substation fault photographs, incl. video-extracted frames) + 4.1 Text (fault-analysis report text)
capability.evaluation_spans: Human-in-the-loop (5 power-industry-engineer 1-5 ratings across 4 quality criteria — the paper's sole evaluation method, no code-based/LLM-judge metric used for the comparative eval itself)
capability.capability_spans: Perception/OCR (AEC-VLM instance — substation fault-image understanding) + Domain Knowledge (substation-equipment/electrical-power instance) + Memory & Context Retention (knowledge-base retrieval-augmented enhancement — BERT keyword extraction + KB lookup)
capability.profession_note: Source: CURATOR benchmark_records.jsonl taxonomy_v2 — Dim5/Dim6 confirmed from full-text (or CURATOR abstract fields for paywalled records).
